content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use expected TypeScript errors to prove invalid state, unsafe narrowing, mutable values, and missing exhaustive cases are rejected.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Compile Negative Tests model · Theory Compile Ne…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 2308 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove original fixtures, pure models, compile-negative tests, loopback faults, and mutation detection; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use expected TypeScript errors to prove invalid state, unsafe narrowing, mutable values, and missing exhaustive cases are rejected.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Compile Negative Tests model · Theory Compile Ne…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing runtime tests proves the type boundary is useful.
